expectations.evidence: Report header identifies 'Type: TypeScript CLI (open-source reconstruction of Claude Code v2.1.88)'. Executive summary describes it as having '~1,921 source files, ~432k lines'. Scaffolding-inventory.json confirms 'project: Open-ClaudeCode' with version 2.1.88 and details the TypeScript/Node.js nature throughout.

Executive summary states 'no CI/CD' explicitly. Dimension 9 (Verify Changes) table shows 'CI pipeline | NO | No .github/workflows/, no CI configuration of any kind'. scaffolding-inventory.json lists CI pipeline under 'missing' items and notes golden_principles.ci_enforced: false. Priority gap 1 notes the absence as a blocking issue.

Executive summary states 'no test infrastructure'. Dimension 9 table explicitly lists: 'Unit tests | NO | No test runner, no test files, no __tests__ directories', 'Integration tests | NO | None', 'E2E tests | NO | None'. scaffolding-inventory.json lists test runner, test files, integration tests, and E2E tests all under 'missing'. Priority gap 2 is titled 'Test Infrastructure (BLOCKING)'.

Report recognizes extensive existing observability: analytics service shipping to Datadog and first-party sinks (dimension 5), session storage with 5,105-line JSONL transcript persistence (dimension 6), VCR recording for test fixtures, asciicast recording, internal logging with Kubernetes namespace detection, diagnostic tracking, cost tracking with per-model token counts and USD costs, and a comprehensive attribution system including commit attribution, tool use tracking, and agent memory snapshots.

Five concrete priority gaps each with specific files needed: (1) tsconfig.json, package.json with devDependencies, working tsc --noEmit; (2) vitest/jest test runner, test utilities for React/Ink components, smoke tests for query pipeline and tool execution; (3) Event emitter at QueryEngine level for structured stream interface; (4) Headless SDK harness wrapping entrypoints/sdk/; (5) Causal chain store linking sessions, tool calls, file changes, and outcomes. All recommendations reference specific source file paths and architectural integration points.
